source.source_excerpt: 3.2.4.2 Flat Rate Margin Flat rate margin is calculated according to the steps as follows: Step 1: Aggregate absolute market value of long positions and absolute market value of short positions separately for each position identified as per instructions in §3.2.2. and compare the total absolute market value of long positions and short positions separately for each sub-margin group of flat rate margin calculations defined on HKEX website31. For example: Sub-group 1 positions InstrumentID Quantity

calculation.formula_description:
3.2.4.2 Flat Rate Margin
Flat rate margin is calculated according to the steps as follows:
Step 1: Aggregate absolute market value of long positions and absolute market
 value of short positions separately for each position identified as per
 instructions in §3.2.2. and compare the total absolute market value of long
 positions and short positions separately for each sub-margin group of flat
 rate margin calculations defined on HKEX website31.
For example:
Sub-group 1 positions
InstrumentID Quantity Absolute market value Absolute market value
 of long positions of short positions
658 <0 in HKD equivalent in HKD equivalent
 0 60,000,000
3606 0
 30,000,000 0
Total
 30,000,000 60,000,000
For the sub-group 1 positions above, the total absolute short market value is higher than
the total absolute long market value. Therefore, all short sub-group 1 positions will be
included in the flat rate margin calculation while all long sub-group 1 positions will be
excluded.
InstrumentID Quantity Contract value Market value
 in HKD Equivalent in HKD Equivalent
658 -10,000,000 -62,000,000 -60,000,000
Sub-group 2 positions
InstrumentID Quantity Contract value Market value
 in HKD Equivalent in HKD Equivalent
3456 10,000
 910,000 750,000
Sub-group 3 positions
InstrumentID Quantity Contract value Market value
 in HKD Equivalent in HKD Equivalent
3457 -50,000
 -360,000 -300,000
Similarly for sub-group 2 and sub-group 3 to take the higher of long and short absolute
market value for each of them, all long sub-group 2 positions and all short sub-group 3
positions will be included in the flat rate margin calculation.
Step 2: Sum the product of absolute position market value and the flat margin rate
 under FieldType 3.
31 https://www.hkex.com.hk/Services/Clearing/Securities/Risk-Management/Margin?sc_lang=en
Step 3: Apply flat rate margin multiplier32 by referring to the “Daily Participant
 Margin Multiplier Report” (“DWH0081C”) to obtain the flat rate margin after
 margin multiplier.
For example, assigning a flat rate margin multiplier of 2 is assigned,
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier
= (60,000,000 × 12% + 750,000 × 30% + 300,000 × 55%) × 2
= 15,180,000

source.source_excerpt: 3.2.5.1 Rounding on Aggregated Market-risk-component Margin Margin rounding is performed on all market risk components according to the steps as follows: Step 1: Calculate aggregated margin derived from market risk components. Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add- on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on = 10,000,000 + 15,180,000 + 266,865 + 550,000 + 2,500,000 + 18,433,039 = 46,929,904 Step

calculation.formula_description:
3.2.5.1 Rounding on Aggregated Market-risk-component Margin
 Margin rounding is performed on all market risk components according to the steps as
 follows:
 Step 1: Calculate aggregated margin derived from market risk components.
 Aggregated market-risk-component margin
 = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-
 on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on
 = 10,000,000 + 15,180,000 + 266,865 + 550,000 + 2,500,000 + 18,433,039
 = 46,929,904
 Step 2: Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter
 stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File (e.g., 10,000 in the sample
 Initial Margin Risk Parameter File).
 In our example, 46,929,904 is to be rounded up to the nearest 10,000.
 Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin = 46,930,000

evidence.quote:
3.2.5.1 Rounding on Aggregated Market-risk-component Margin
 Margin rounding is performed on all market risk components according to the steps as
 follows:
 Step 1: Calculate aggregated margin derived from market risk components.
 Aggregated market-risk-component margin
 = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-
 on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on
 = 10,000,000 + 15,180,000 + 266,865 + 550,000 + 2,500,000 + 18,433,039
 = 46,929,904
 Step 2: Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter
 stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File (e.g., 10,000 in the sample
 Initial Margin Risk Parameter File).
 In our example, 46,929,904 is to be rounded up to the nearest 10,000.
 Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin = 46,930,000

test_design_hints.gherkin_intent: 3.2.5.1 Rounding on Aggregated Market-risk-component Margin Margin rounding is performed on all market risk components according to the steps as follows: Step 1: Calculate aggregated margin derived from market risk components. Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add- on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on = 10,000,000 + 15,180,000 + 266,865 + 550,000 + 2,500,000 + 18,433,039 = 46,929,904 Step 2: Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File (e.g., 10,000 in the sample Initial Margin Risk Parameter File). In our example, 46,929,904 is to be rounded up to the nearest 10,000. Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin = 46,930,000

source.source_excerpt: 3.2.5.2 Consideration on Favorable MTM Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement)41 could be determined by the steps as follows: Step 1: Calculate favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) by using the formula as follows: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement)42 = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio 39 The Holiday_Factor parameter is 0.7320508075 which implies the number of consecutive holidays is 3. Please refer to §2.2 for the conversion methodology. 40 Favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually

calculation.formula_description:
3.2.5.2 Consideration on Favorable MTM
 Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement)41 could be determined by the steps as follows:
 Step 1: Calculate favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) by using the formula as
 follows:
 Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement)42
 = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
39 The Holiday_Factor parameter is 0.7320508075 which implies the number of consecutive holidays is 3. Please refer to §2.2 for
the conversion methodology.
40 Favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually exclusive. See §3.2.5.2 for details.
41 Favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually exclusive. In the MTM and Margin Requirement Report, absolute value of
favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) will be shown.
42 The aggregated value of HKD equivalent contract value and HKD equivalent market value. Numbers are rounded off on position
level. Please refer to Appendix 4.6 for calculation logic that involve multiple currencies in the portfolio.
= (-300,550,000) – (-287,850,000)
 = -12,700,000
 The negative number refers to a MTM requirement, which its absolute value will be added
 after applying margin credit (See §3.2.6.1). In such case, favorable MTM is zero.
 Step 2: Deduct favorable MTM from rounded aggregated market-risk-component
 margin to derive net margin.
 Net margin
 = Maximum (0 , Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin – Favorable MTM)
 = Maximum [0 , (46,930,000 – 0)]
 = 46,930,000

evidence.quote:
3.2.5.2 Consideration on Favorable MTM
 Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement)41 could be determined by the steps as follows:
 Step 1: Calculate favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) by using the formula as
 follows:
 Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement)42
 = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
39 The Holiday_Factor parameter is 0.7320508075 which implies the number of consecutive holidays is 3. Please refer to §2.2 for
the conversion methodology.
40 Favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually exclusive. See §3.2.5.2 for details.
41 Favorable MTM and MTM requirement are mutually exclusive. In the MTM and Margin Requirement Report, absolute value of
favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) will be shown.
42 The aggregated value of HKD equivalent contract value and HKD equivalent market value. Numbers are rounded off on position
level. Please refer to Appendix 4.6 for calculation logic that involve multiple currencies in the portfolio.
= (-300,550,000) – (-287,850,000)
 = -12,700,000
 The negative number refers to a MTM requirement, which its absolute value will be added
 after applying margin credit (See §3.2.6.1). In such case, favorable MTM is zero.
 Step 2: Deduct favorable MTM from rounded aggregated market-risk-component
 margin to derive net margin.
 Net margin
 = Maximum (0 , Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin – Favorable MTM)
 = Maximum [0 , (46,930,000 – 0)]
 = 46,930,000

source.source_excerpt: 3.2.5.3 Application of Margin Credit A margin credit (normally 5,000,000)43 is granted to each CP and applied for margin calculation44. Net margin after credit is calculated as follows: Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)] = Maximum [0 , (46,930,000 – 5,000,000)] = 41,930,000

calculation.formula_description:
3.2.5.3 Application of Margin Credit
 A margin credit (normally 5,000,000)43 is granted to each CP and applied for margin
 calculation44.
 Net margin after credit is calculated as follows:
 Net margin after credit
 = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]
 = Maximum [0 , (46,930,000 – 5,000,000)]
 = 41,930,000

evidence.quote:

 Net margin after credit
 = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

 = 41,930,000

source.section: 3.2.8 Derive Total MTM and Margin Requirement from Results under §3.2.5 & §3.2.6
source.source_excerpt: 3.2.8 Derive Total MTM and Margin Requirement from Results under §3.2.5 & §3.2.6 Eventually, total MTM and margin requirement could be derived by adding the net margin after credit to other risk components. Total MTM and margin requirement = Net margin after credit + Other risk components = Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on = 41,930,000 + 12,700,000 + 487,332 + 12,000,000 + 600,000 = 67,717,332 46 Please note that the credit ri

calculation.formula_description:
3.2.8 Derive Total MTM and Margin Requirement from Results under §3.2.5 & §3.2.6
Eventually, total MTM and margin requirement could be derived by adding the net margin
after credit to other risk components.
Total MTM and margin requirement
= Net margin after credit + Other risk components
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on +
 Ad-hoc add-on
= 41,930,000 + 12,700,000 + 487,332 + 12,000,000 + 600,000
= 67,717,332
46 Please note that the credit risk add-on will not be applicable upon the launch of VaR Platform. HKSCC will notify CPs before the
implementation.
4. APPENDIX
calculation.source_section: 3.2.8 Derive Total MTM and Margin Requirement from Results under §3.2.5 & §3.2.6

evidence.quote:
3.2.8 Derive Total MTM and Margin Requirement from Results under §3.2.5 & §3.2.6
Eventually, total MTM and margin requirement could be derived by adding the net margin
after credit to other risk components.
Total MTM and margin requirement
= Net margin after credit + Other risk components
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on +
 Ad-hoc add-on
= 41,930,000 + 12,700,000 + 487,332 + 12,000,000 + 600,000
= 67,717,332
46 Please note that the credit risk add-on will not be applicable upon the launch of VaR Platform. HKSCC will notify CPs before the
implementation.
4. APPENDIX

HKSCC will notify CPs before the implementation. 4. APPENDIX
